brief_summary: Horizontal sound-character spectrum, deepest/most-damped on the left to brightest/most-ringy on the right. Tick-with-circle markers for UPE, POM, nylon, LY nylon, all-PC. A wider amber bar marks the 'PC top + nylon bottom' modern default in the middle-right. ABS sits at the far right with a dashed muted circle and 'avoid' note.
data_sources: switch-housings-compared.mdx § 'Why housing material matters' — 'A harder plastic lets the chamber ring at a higher pitch and for longer; a softer plastic absorbs more of the ring'
switch-housings-compared.mdx § 'Nylon' — 'warm, muted, sound-dampening, and forgiving ... the Cherry sound'
switch-housings-compared.mdx § 'Polycarbonate' — 'PC top, nylon bottom ... modern default'
switch-housings-compared.mdx § 'POM' — 'muted, slightly hollow at the top of travel ... distinctively dense at the bottom-out'

switch-housings-compared.mdx § 'The rest, briefly' — 'UHMWPE ... the deepest and most damped ... LY nylon ... brighter top-end than PA66 but still warmer than PC ... ABS housings exist almost entirely in budget switches and should be avoided'
